geography.data_source: Census 2011 ward boundary shapefiles, North East Delhi district. Covers Mustafabad, Seelampur, Bhajanpura, Jaffrabad, Karawal Nagar.

demographics.data_source: Census 2011 North East Delhi district tables. Highest density zone in Delhi (44210/sqkm). Density = 2100000 / 47.5 ≈ 44210. Large household size (5.2) reflects joint family structures and migrant shared housing. Lowest literacy rate among Delhi zones.

income_profile.data_source: NSSO 78th Round MPCE distribution (North East Delhi district). Heavily left-skewed — lowest median income zone in Delhi. Highest informal employment (0.72) in Delhi, dominated by daily wage and construction work.

commute_profile.data_source: DMRC Origin-Destination Survey 2022, Census 2011 Table D-13. Limited metro coverage — only Pink Line touches the southwestern edge. Most residents bus-dependent. Longest average commute time in Delhi (55min) due to poor connectivity and distance to employment centres.

sensitivity_parameters.data_source: Composite scores. Highest fare sensitivity in Delhi (0.91) — lowest income + longest commute + bus dependency. Very low collective action threshold (0.28) — strong community and labour networks, frequent local agitations. Lowest institutional trust in railways (0.42) reflecting poor service coverage. High political mobilisation (0.82) from ECI 2024 North East Delhi constituency data.

social_network_parameters.data_source: Estimated from Census 2011 household density and IHDS-II social capital module. Highest clustering (0.76) and connections (48) in Delhi — dense residential colonies with strong community bonds. Very low cross-income bridging (0.07) reflecting income homogeneity.

informal_economy_nodes.name: Seelampur Scrap Market
informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Estimated from MCD waste management reports. Seelampur is one of Asia's largest e-waste recycling markets. Employment figure includes informal waste pickers and recyclers.

informal_economy_nodes.name: Bhajanpura Market
informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Estimated from MCD market data. Local residential market and small manufacturing cluster (leather goods, garments).

informal_economy_nodes.name: Mustafabad Local Market
informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Estimated from local survey data. Serves extremely dense Mustafabad neighbourhood, primarily food and daily essentials.

informal_economy_nodes.name: Karawal Nagar Market
informal_economy_nodes.data_source: Estimated from MCD data. Outer fringe market with lower transit dependency due to predominantly walking catchment.

tier1_agent_archetype_weights.data_source: AGENT_SPEC.md Dense Low-Income template (lines 716-727). Sum = 1.00. Daily wage workers (0.28) + migrant workers (0.18) = 0.46 of population is extremely transit-sensitive labour. Street vendors (0.12) concentrated around Seelampur and Bhajanpura markets.

tier2_agent_types.data_source: Estimated from MCD ward councillor count (10 wards), labour union registration data, and community organisation surveys. Higher union representative count (4) reflects strong construction and waste worker unions in the zone.
